title: Boat Rental in Bodrum: Costs, Tips and Best Boats for 2026
html:
Boat rental in Bodrum offers a gateway to the shimmering Aegean, with prices from 2,000 TRY for a half-day motorboat. Depart from Milta Bodrum Marina or Yalikavak Marina and explore secluded bays. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners and a wide fleet of boats.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Bodrum?

Prices range from 1,500 TRY for a shared tour to 15,000 TRY for a full-day crewed yacht. The cost depends on boat size, season, and duration. Below is a typical price breakdown:

  • Shared gulet tour (full day, 8-12 guests): from 1,500 TRY per person
  • Small motorboat (half day, 4-6 guests): from 2,500 TRY
  • Catamaran (full day, 8-12 guests): from 8,000 TRY
  • Luxury crewed yacht (full day, 8-10 guests): from 12,000 TRY

Remember to factor in fuel and mooring fees. You can book a charter online through BoatHire24, where prices are transparent and owners verified.

Where to set off in Bodrum?

Bodrum has several excellent marinas and bays for departure. Milta Bodrum Marina, located in the town centre, offers modern facilities and access to many charter boats. Yalikavak Marina, a short drive west, is a popular base for luxury yachts and speedboats. For a quieter start, consider Turgutreis Marina or the fishing harbour at Gumusluk. From these points, you can reach scenic spots such as Camel Beach, Rabbit Island, or the old city of Knidos.

Which boat to rent?

  1. Speedboat (e.g. Olympic 490), ideal for a fast day trip to nearby bays; seats 4-6 guests; from 2,500 TRY half-day
  2. Small motorboat (e.g. 5m centre console), perfect for exploring Orak Island and Paradise Bay; seats 4-5; from 2,000 TRY half-day
  3. Gulet, traditional wooden sailing yacht, great for group cruises to Pabuç Burnu; seats 8-12; from 6,000 TRY full-day
  4. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 40), stable and spacious, ideal for families to reach Akvaryum Bay; seats 8-12; from 8,000 TRY full-day
  5. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 50), premium experience with crew, suitable for special occasions; seats 8-10; from 12,000 TRY full-day

For example, the Olympic 490 Rhodes is a popular speedboat. View this speedboat listing on BoatHire24 for details and availability.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Bodrum?

For boats up to 15 hp or 6 metres, no licence is needed from many rental operators. Larger vessels may require an ICC or equivalent. Always confirm exact requirements with the operator before booking. The high season runs June to August, when the Meltemi wind can pick up in the afternoons, so plan your itinerary accordingly.

For a quieter day on the water, depart before 10am to avoid the Meltemi and enjoy calm seas around Orak Island.

At a glance

  • Price range: 1,500, 15,000 TRY
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: 4 hours (half day) to 8 hours (full day)
  • Typical guest capacity: 4-12
  • Currency: Turkish Lira (TRY)

Questions about boat rental in Bodrum

Can I rent a boat in Bodrum without a licence?

Small motorboats under 15 hp or 6 metres are often available without a licence, but you must check with the rental company.

What is the best time of day to rent a boat in Bodrum?

Morning departures (around 9am) are recommended to avoid the strong Meltemi wind and enjoy calm seas.

Are self-drive boats available in Bodrum?

Yes, many operators offer self-drive boats, especially small motorboats and speedboats, with a short briefing included.

What islands can I visit from Bodrum by boat?

Popular destinations include Orak Island, Rabbit Island, and the Greek island of Kos on certain tours.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Bodrum or list your own. To start planning your 2026 adventure, browse the listings. If you own a boat in Bodrum, consider becoming a host on BoatHire24 and earn extra income.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Bodrum and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
